state_checks.responsive_fit: verified at desktop and compact500 through screenshot artifacts plus dump-dom layout metrics
state_checks.primary_action_visibility: button is visible in the desktop first fold, but the before hierarchy does not make it feel like the next decisive action
state_checks.loading_empty_error: not applicable to this static calibration fixture
state_checks.focus_keyboard_hover: not claimed; no pointer or keyboard interaction artifact was captured

required_findings: The before variant makes the full-width summary, metric strip, review rows, and next-action panel compete for attention instead of naming one decision object.
The summary repeats the lead count as a second full-width card, so the first actionable review row arrives lower and feels optional.
The desktop screenshot shows competent spacing and typography, but the information order is still overview-first rather than decision-first.
false_positive_guards: Do not score this above the low 80s because the first fold is still dominated by equal overview surfaces rather than a prioritized work object.
Do not treat the local fixture as proof of production data, authenticated routing, backend integration, or real user workflow behavior.
Do not claim exact 390px phone coverage; compact responsive evidence is explicitly limited to 500x844 in this run.
